The only condition supported by the evidence for autosomal dominant inheritance is Hailey-Hailey disease, also known as benign familial pemphigus. Although neither option explicitly names this disorder, white sponge nevus is sometimes associated with autosomal dominant patterns; however, due to lack of supporting evidence in the text, no definitive choice can be made from the listed options without further clarification.
